company.name: Manus
company.tagline: From a 2-minute demo video to $100M ARR in nine months — and a $2B Meta deal Beijing killed
company.summary: Manus is the cleanest example of a single demo video resetting a category. A March 6, 2025 product video pulled two million people onto a waitlist, sent invite codes to $7,000 on the resale market, and forced every major US lab to ship an 'agent' answer within twelve weeks. Behind the viral surface: a serial founder on his third company, a chief scientist who had been shipping consumer AI since high school, and a deliberate choice to launch in English on X rather than at home in Chinese.

events.title: Butterfly Effect founded
events.description: Xiao Hong incorporates Butterfly Effect Technology Co. in Beijing (April 2022) after exiting Wuhan Nightingale Technology. ZhenFund-supported launch follows in mid-2022. Initial product is Monica.im, an AI browser-extension copilot targeting overseas markets first.

events.title: Seed round (ZhenFund)
events.description: ZhenFund-led seed at roughly $14M post-money. The first of two ZhenFund rounds before any product traction is visible publicly.

events.title: Angel round (ZhenFund)
events.description: Second ZhenFund round at roughly $50M post-money. Monica's Chrome extension hits early scale in non-Chinese markets.

events.title: Monica passes 1M users
events.description: Monica.im, Butterfly Effect's first product, becomes one of the more-installed AI browser extensions globally — overwhelmingly outside mainland China.

events.title: Series A — $85M valuation
events.description: HSG (formerly Sequoia China), Tencent, ZhenFund, and Meituan co-founder Wang Huiwen invest. Post-money around $85M. The round funds the pivot from extension to autonomous agent.

events.title: Internal Manus alpha to investors
events.description: Closed alpha shown to existing backers in the two weeks before public launch. Internal benchmarks against OpenAI Deep Research are the basis of the launch positioning.

events.title: Manus public demo on X
events.description: Yichao 'Peak' Ji posts a 2-minute demo video on X. The video shows a single agent searching apartments, populating a spreadsheet, comparing flight prices, and writing a finished travel doc — end-to-end, no human intervention. Crosses 1M views in under 20 hours.

events.title: Waitlist hits 2M
events.description: Within 48 hours of the demo, the invite-only waitlist crosses two million. Discord server passes 138,000 members in the same week. Invite codes start appearing on Xianyu (China) and eBay.

events.title: TechCrunch 'second DeepSeek?'
events.description: TechCrunch publishes 'Manus probably isn't China's second DeepSeek moment' — and the framing itself becomes the dominant frame. Every major Western outlet covers Manus inside seven days.

events.title: Alibaba Qwen partnership
events.description: Manus and Alibaba's Qwen team announce a partnership to bring all Manus features onto domestic Chinese models and infrastructure. The fastest 'we have a China-compute story' answer of any 2025 viral AI product.

events.title: Paid plans + iOS app
events.description: Starter at $39/mo (3,900 credits), Pro at $199/mo (19,900 credits). iOS app ships the same day. Backend is upgraded to Claude 3.7 Sonnet. 25 days from viral demo to monetization.

events.title: Series B — $75M / $500M
events.description: Benchmark leads, Tencent / ZhenFund / HSG follow on. Post-money around $500M — roughly six times the November Series A. Chetan Puttagunta joins the board.

events.title: US CFIUS-style review of Benchmark
events.description: Treasury reviews Benchmark's investment in Manus under outbound-investment rules covering Chinese AI. The first regulatory shadow over the company's cap table — and the trigger for the Singapore conversation.

events.title: Singapore HQ + China layoffs
events.description: Manus moves headquarters to Singapore, relocates roughly 40 core technical staff, and lays off most of the 120-person Beijing team. The product is shelved on mainland China entirely. A single move trades domestic distribution for Western chip and capital access.

events.title: Wide Research launch
events.description: Manus introduces Wide Research — clusters of 100+ parallel Manus instances working a single task. The architectural answer to 'is this just a Claude wrapper?' Bloomberg covers the launch the same day.

events.title: $90M annualized revenue
events.description: Bloomberg reports Manus has reached a $90M annualized revenue run-rate. Five months from paid-plan launch. Sacra publishes a same-week deep-dive that becomes the de facto reference.

events.title: MIT Tech Review 35-under-35
events.description: MIT Technology Review names Yichao 'Peak' Ji to its Innovators Under 35 list. The first Western institutional validation of a Manus founder by name — and the moment Ji's X account becomes a media object in its own right.

events.title: Manus 1.5
events.description: Major upgrade: tasks finish ~4× faster, full-stack web app build-and-deploy lands inside the agent, internal benchmarks show 15% quality improvement. Triggers 20%+ month-over-month revenue growth.

events.title: Manus 1.6 + $100M ARR
events.description: Manus 1.6 ships with Max performance mode, mobile dev, and Design View. Bloomberg reports the $100M ARR / $125M revenue run-rate disclosure on Dec 17, 2025 — Manus's blog post is dated within the same window. Both numbers fire into the lead-up to the Meta announcement.

events.title: Meta acquires Manus
events.description: Meta announces the acquisition of Butterfly Effect at a reported valuation between $2B and $3B. Roughly 100 Manus employees set to relocate to Meta's Singapore office. Xiao Hong reportedly slated to become a Meta VP.

events.title: China opens national-security review
events.description: China's Ministry of Commerce announces it will examine the Meta deal under technology-export and data-security rules. A six-month limbo begins.

events.title: Exit bans on co-founders
events.description: Xiao Hong and Yichao Ji are reportedly barred from leaving China while the review proceeds (FT/Reuters reporting Mar 25–26). The acquisition is now in active jeopardy.

events.title: China blocks the Meta deal
events.description: China's National Development and Reform Commission orders the parties to withdraw the transaction, citing tech-export and national-security concerns. The first Chinese-origin AI deal of this size killed at the regulatory level.

platforms.name: X (Twitter)
platforms.role: The launchpad and the entire viral inflection
platforms.description: Manus chose to launch in English on X — not in Chinese on Weibo. The March 6, 2025 demo video posted by Yichao Ji on X is the single asset the entire arc pivots on. Every Western outlet picked the story up from there.
platforms.catalyst: Yichao Ji's launch demo (Mar 6, 2025) — a 2-minute walkthrough of Manus completing a multi-step task end-to-end. Crossed one million views in under twenty hours and put Manus on every AI Twitter timeline within a weekend.
platforms.whenItWorks: When you have a 90-second demo that fully shows the new behavior — and a credible founder face attached to the post. The format rewards 'show, don't pitch'
platforms.whenItDoesnt: If your product needs explanation before the demo makes sense. X demos that need a thread to set up rarely break out

platforms.name: YouTube
platforms.description: Yichao Ji has done a small number of high-quality long-form interviews — the MIT Technology Review profile and an Asia Tech Lens conversation are the durable references. YouTube is where the 'is this just a Claude wrapper?' debate gets answered with an actual founder voice.
platforms.catalyst: Yichao Ji's MIT Tech Review Innovators Under 35 profile (Sep 2025) — the first Western institutional validation of a Manus founder by name, and the moment skeptics had something other than a demo video to cite.
platforms.whenItWorks: When you have a chief-scientist founder who can answer technical pushback in real time on camera. Long-form is where wrappers get exposed and architecture gets credit
platforms.whenItDoesnt: Don't go on long-form podcasts when your product is still in its first 60 days of public scrutiny. The format will surface gaps faster than your roadmap can fill them

platforms.name: Hacker News
platforms.description: Manus broke containment to HN in the same March week as the X demo. The HN thread became the place where the 'wrapper or real architecture' question got debated — and where a leaked system prompt gave critics ammunition. The Wide Research launch in July was Manus's deliberate HN-credibility answer.
platforms.catalyst: Wide Research launch (Jul 31, 2025) — 100+ parallel agent architecture. Front-paged on HN with substantive technical comments. Two weeks later: $90M ARR disclosure. The HN signal preceded the revenue news.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the skepticism is real and the answer can be defended in technical detail. HN rewards 'we built this thing because the existing approach hits this specific limit'
platforms.whenItDoesnt: Don't post a marketing demo on HN. The crowd will surface the architectural questions you didn't want asked, and you'll spend a week defending instead of selling

platforms.name: Reddit
platforms.description: r/ManusAI and adjacent subs became the place where the credit-system pricing controversy played out. Reddit is also where invite-code seekers and resellers congregated in March — a real-time signal of demand far ahead of any official disclosure.
platforms.catalyst: No single moment. The credit-system threads through April–May 2025 forced Manus to clarify pricing language and shape later UI changes. Slow-burn pressure that the company eventually had to address.
platforms.whenItWorks: When you have a pricing model users will scrutinize publicly. Reddit makes the spreadsheet visible — better to read it than ignore it
platforms.whenItDoesnt: If you try to seed positive threads, the community detects it inside a week. Reddit is community-trust dependent and astroturfing dies fast

platforms.name: LinkedIn
platforms.description: LinkedIn became material around the Series B and the Meta announcement. The Benchmark partner posts and the Meta acquisition statement re-shared by Singaporean tech press are the two LinkedIn assets that did real work. Otherwise inert.
platforms.catalyst: The Meta acquisition announcement (Dec 29, 2025) — re-shared across the Singapore VC and Meta employee networks within hours. The first time LinkedIn carried meaningful Manus narrative weight.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the announcement is itself news — funding round, acquisition, hire of a notable name. LinkedIn doesn't make news, it amplifies
platforms.whenItDoesnt: As an acquisition channel for an invite-only consumer agent — near zero ROI. Skip the content calendar

platforms.name: Instagram
platforms.description: Manus has not built an Instagram presence. The audience-product fit is wrong — agentic AI demos play as videos on X and YouTube, not as IG carousels — and the founders themselves don't post there.
platforms.catalyst: Useful as a counter-example. A product whose entire viral moment is a screen-recording of an agent working has nothing to gain from a square-format visual feed.
platforms.whenItWorks: For consumer-facing creator tools or visual-first products. Manus is neither
platforms.whenItDoesnt: B2B-style agent products with technical demos. Skip it without guilt
